details: Started selective integration of final_kit into OV10 control plane while preserving existing higher-trust code and docs.
Planned Windows watchdog variants and repo monitor adoption with documented operator-managed risk.
Added OV10-specific .codex memory, backlog, task graph, prompts, specialist roles, scripts, ADR 0007, and runbook updates; validation passed with pytest, ruff, and compileall.

Added workbook-based portfolio and book config loading with TOML fallback, normalized reference books into current contracts, updated runbook and ADR 0008, and validated through loader, allocation, and persistence tests.
Added local synthetic workbook generation, CLI commands, tests, specialist ownership, ADR 0009, and scenario-matrix support for perturbation testing.

Created and deployed the standalone Apps Script debug bridge, confirmed web-app and execution-api entry points, and narrowed the remaining blocker to one-time Google authorization or execution-path permissions.

Corrected the obfuscated remote key derivation, fetched the live payload `v61_controle_64`, created a debug workbook copy, added reproducible CLI capture commands, and stored hash-verified artifacts under var/online_runtime/reference.

Added reproducible payload-catalog tooling, generated the first function inventory for `v61_controle_64`, documented the high-value rule clusters, and moved the next focus to broker attribution in the `cash_brokers` module.

Implemented governed broker attribution for corporate action cash, removed the system-account warning from the real fixture, added ADR 0011, updated operational expectations, and validated attributed and unresolved paths with tests.
Confirmed that OV10 still had only one historical commit, 7 tracked files, and a large active untracked surface, creating repository-loss risk despite a configured remote.
Added ADR 0019, Git checkpoint policy, local sync-audit automation, scheduled Windows reminder tooling, and control-plane rules for checkpoint cadence and remote-sync hygiene.
